dimensions_ft._note: rotation_deg=0 means long axis runs N-S. width_ew is the E-W extent.

corners_local_ft._note: Local feet coordinate system: origin at SW property corner, +X=east, +Y=north

corners_wgs84._note: Decimal degrees, WGS84. Use these for survey staking and GIS overlays.

binding_constraint.note: NE corner remains the binding east-setback point on the cul-de-sac curve; modeled clearance updated after shifting the pad ~8 ft southeast (May 2026) to reduce graded pad / skirt intrusion on preserved SW and N oak CRZs. Re-stake NE after move; survey error >2 ft can still push into violation.

remove.removal_reason: Trunk inside shop footprint (no other option preserves it without 13+ ft variance and high collateral damage to OAK_N)
remove.felling_window: October-March (avoid oak wilt vector season Apr-Jul)
remove.wood_disposition: Mill for slabs/beams; portable sawyer ~$1.50-2.50/board ft

preserve.status: After pad shift SE (May 2026): trunk-to-footprint edge ~16 ft; canopy may still overhang — roots outside 5' danger zone
After pad shift SE: north edge of pad farther from trunk — ~28 ft edge clearance to footprint
After pad shift SE: west edge of pad farther from trunk

_arborist_methodology.30_percent_root_rule: A healthy mature tree typically survives loss of up to 30% of roots. Above that, mortality risk is substantial.
_arborist_methodology.danger_zone_derivation: For one-sided construction, danger_zone = dripline_radius / 2. Construction inside this radius exceeds 30% root impact for the affected side.
_arborist_methodology.owner_acceptance: Owner accepts canopy intrusion (roots intact) on retained trees. Owner does NOT accept placements that risk killing them.

local_origin.description: SW corner of the parcel (Lot 3 Blk 1 Eagle Creek Estates)
local_origin.convention: x_east = +X feet east of origin; y_north = +Y feet north of origin

reference_geometry.lidar_crs: EPSG:26915 (NAD83 / UTM Zone 15N), elevation meters above NAVD88

setback_definitions._east_note: East setback follows the curving cul-de-sac arc; bows west (toward parcel interior) at higher latitudes. See parcel_and_setbacks_mymaps.kml for exact geometry.

previous_options_dropped.description: 32x40 kitty-corner NE of house, ~40' from house
previous_options_dropped.status: DROPPED — superseded by Option F (20+ feet further north, near cul-de-sac)

previous_options_dropped.description: 32x40 shifted 25' further north than Option A

previous_options_dropped.status: DROPPED — superseded by Option F (see shop_plan_option_c.kml for original geometry)

supersedes.change_summary: Decision evolved from earlier A/B/C placements (all rejected). Option F locked near cul-de-sac; pad center revised to (140, 380) ft local (May 2026) — ~8 ft southeast of prior (132, 388) — to pull graded pad / skirt away from preserved SW and N oak CRZs while keeping OAK_SE removal inside the footprint. Re-verify NE binding with survey after staking.

rejected_alternatives_for_reference.description: 40W rotated, snug against cul-de-sac between SE and N oaks
rejected_alternatives_for_reference.rejection_reason: Required ~13' variance AND violated SE/N danger zones — trees lost anyway

rejected_alternatives_for_reference.description: 32W x 40L between SE and N, no oak removal
rejected_alternatives_for_reference.rejection_reason: Required ~28' variance (42% intrusion into 67' setback) — denial likely

rejected_alternatives_for_reference.description: 40W x 32L rotated, snuggled into far west of buildable area
rejected_alternatives_for_reference.rejection_reason: All oaks preserved BUT shop ~50' further from cul-de-sac, OAK SW exactly at 5' danger threshold (construction reality damages it anyway), defeats new approach driveway purpose

owner_constraints.shop_dimensions: FIXED at 32x40 or 40x32 (rotated). No other sizes considered.
owner_constraints.trailer_storage: 2022 Grand Design Imagine XLS 23BHE: 10'10" clearance target, 23' long. Absolute minimum 11' overhead door; 12' preferred for comfortable clearance and common sizing.
owner_constraints.tree_protection: Belt-and-suspenders protection (temp fencing, etc.) explicitly DECLINED
owner_constraints.variance_preference: Zero-variance solution strongly preferred. Application possible but politically expensive.
owner_constraints.existing_planting_credit: Owner has planted 100+ trees on the property. Net forest stewardship is positive.

validation_targets._purpose: Self-check criteria for any code that produces shop geometry from this spec
validation_targets.all_preserved_oaks_safe: For each oak in trees.preserve: edge_dist_to_shop_ft >= danger_zone_ft
validation_targets.no_oak_trunks_inside_shop: For each oak in trees.preserve: trunk location is NOT inside shop polygon
validation_targets.ne_corner_setback: NE corner east_setback clearance >= 2.0 ft (must remain compliant)
validation_targets.removed_oak_trunk_inside: OAK_SE trunk SHOULD be inside shop polygon (it's being removed)
validation_targets.foundation_type_specified: spec.foundation.type must be present and == 'stem_wall' (no extended pad model)
validation_targets.no_pad_extension_in_root_calcs: Tree edge_dist_to_shop_ft values must be measured from shop polygon edge, not from a pad+taper edge

foundation.description: Stem wall foundation where exterior face of the foundation IS the visible wall above grade. No graded pad extension beyond shop footprint. Confirmed with excavator.
foundation.footing_depth_note: Below MN frost line (~42-48 inches). Confirm exact depth with local building official / soils report.
foundation.driveway_target_grade_note: Stem-wall default: finish elevation is lifted so the modeled chord (east property line +10′ road DEM sample → east edge of the concrete working pad per east_working_pad_ft) matches ≈ this driveway_target_grade_pct unless slab_finish_elev_ft_navd88 is set. Positive % ⇒ slab finish above the road sample (same sign convention as generate_viz driveway_grade_chord_road_to_east_pad_edge). Override lift with foundation.slab_finish_elev_ft_navd88 for a fixed FF. Env SHOP_TOPO_DRIVEWAY_TARGET_GRADE_PCT overrides the numeric target when the lift path is used.
foundation.slab_finish_elev_note: When set to a number (NAVD88 ft), earthwork cut/fill, graded DEM, and viz use this finish floor; driveway_target_grade_pct remains design documentation—the reported driveway_drain grade is the actual chord % at this slab elevation.
foundation.east_working_pad_note: Exterior concrete east of the east wall only (same N-S as shop). east_working_pad_grade_pct is rise/run×100 in +X (east): negative = finish drops east to shed water (e.g. −5% over 25′ ≈ 1.25′). Not the superseded 5′ graded pad + 2:1 taper CRZ model.
foundation.construction_buffer_note: Excavator working room only — TEMPORARY disturbance during construction, restored to natural grade after backfill. NOT a permanent pad extension. Tree root impact analysis should NOT include this buffer as a permanent disturbed footprint.
foundation.supersedes_pad_model: Earlier topo analysis modeled a 5 ft graded pad extension with 2:1 taper down to natural grade. That approach is REJECTED for this project because the tapered slope footprint extends into the danger zones of OAK SW, OAK W, and OAK N — the trees the placement was designed to preserve. Foundation-wall approach keeps the permanently disturbed footprint to the shop boundary only.
implications_for_topo_analysis: Cut/fill volumes calculated for the 32x40 shop footprint only (1280 sq ft), not for an extended pad with taper (~2300+ sq ft under the old model).
Finish floor elevation can be set HIGHER than a balanced-cut pad model suggests, because we are not flattening a larger area to achieve a uniform pad surface.
Tree root impact distances are measured from the shop foundation footprint edge, not from a pad+taper edge.
Imported fill volume is substantially lower (rough estimate: 40-60% reduction vs the pad model).
Driveway grade calculation: shop 'pad finish elevation' now refers to the top-of-foundation / interior floor elevation, not a flattened exterior apron elevation.
Backfill against the foundation wall on the uphill (west) side is engineered fill, compacted in lifts. The wall is designed to retain it; no slope is needed.
what_NOT_to_do_in_topo_model: Do not extend the disturbed footprint 5 ft beyond the shop on any side.
Do not apply a 2:1 or 3:1 taper slope from a pad edge.
Do not compute cut/fill against a shop+pad polygon — use the shop polygon only.
Do not flag OAK SW, OAK W, or OAK N as in-danger based on pad extension geometry.
